steps.title: Recognize the cost equation structure
steps.content:
The equation $f = 2.50x + 7.00y$ represents a total cost formula.

The general form is:
Total = (unit price) × (quantity) + (unit price) × (quantity)

In this context, each coefficient (2.50 and 7.00) represents a unit price, and each variable ($x$ and $y$) represents a quantity.

steps.title: Determine what 7.00 represents
steps.content: Since 7.00 is the coefficient multiplying $y$, and $y$ represents the number of salads, we know that 7.00 is the unit price of a salad. In other words, 7.00 is the price, in dollars, of one salad. That matches choice B — B is correct.
